compiled_state.author: Joe Oakhart

comments.author: Joe Oakhart
comments.body:
ROOT CAUSE PROVEN + FIXED (rebar-debug protocol, worktree fix-review-sidecar-race).

MECHANISM (runtime-confirmed): _prune_queue_events (enrich_drain.py) ran a raw UNLOCKED whole-index git rm + git commit, outside the unified store write lock (I5) and with no pathspec. In the [agents] drain child it races locked append_event writes two ways: (1) sweep-and-strand — its whole-index commit sweeps a concurrent writer's staged SIGNATURE/REVIEW_RESULT blob into the prune commit; the writer's own commit then fails 'nothing to commit' and the failure path unlinks the file from the worktree though it is in HEAD; (2) 'cannot lock ref HEAD: is at X but expected Y'. Both swallowed by sidecar.emit (->sidecar_emitted=False) and attest.sign_plan_review (->unsigned). Isolated-store experiment (remote OFF, so branch-divergence excluded): 33/360 concurrent writes swept-and-stranded on buggy code; 0 with the fix. Explains why [dev]-only concurrency did NOT reproduce (no drain => no unlocked committer).

FIX: route the deletion through the canonical locked, pathspec-scoped event_append.delete_events — the exact pattern already applied to the twin prunes plan_review/sidecar.py::prune and overlap/digest_sidecar.py (bug malevolent-emigratory-umbrette). _prune_queue_events was the un-hardened twin. One-function change.

RED->GREEN: new held-out regression tests/interfaces/store/test_enrich_drain_prune_concurrency.py — concurrent locked writers vs the real prune committer must lose zero. RED on origin/main (22/250 lost), GREEN with the fix, mutation-checked (reverting the fix returns it to RED). make lint + make typecheck green; make test running. Fix-safety: restoration (I5), no new dep (delete_events exists), no test weakened.

SIBLING (Step-7 sweep): purge_bridge.py has the same unlocked whole-index commit on the tracker — filed as grassgreen-clotty-duck (discovered_from), lower exposure (manual operator command), separate fix.

comments.author: Joe Oakhart
comments.body: [operator-attested] CI GREEN + LANDED. Gerrit change 743 (https://rebar.solutions.navateam.com/c/rebar/+/743): Verified=+1 AND LLM-Review=+1 (PASS, 2 advisory non-blocking), 0 unresolved. All CI jobs green on the recheck run: CI(ubuntu py3.11/3.12/3.13), CI(macos py3.13), golden-path(clean install), all optionality/packaging, require-resolvable-rebar-ticket. Submitted -> status MERGED; landed on origin/main as commit 9938a8aab. Locally on the CI Python (3.12): make lint OK, make typecheck 'Success: no issues found in 327 source files', store suite (307) + the new regression test green. (The 3 test_config_* failures seen in a local full run were a machine-local ~/.config/rebar/config.toml artifact — pass with a clean HOME as CI has — unrelated to this change.)

compiled_state.description:
# Concurrent review-plan silently drops the plan-review attestation: enrich_drain._prune_queue_events uses an unlocked whole-index git commit that races locked store writes

## Summary

Under `[agents]`, every `review-plan` finish triggers the opportunistic enrichment drain, whose
`rebar.llm.enrich_drain._prune_queue_events` (`src/rebar/llm/enrich_drain.py:191-207`) stages and
commits with a **raw, unlocked, whole-index** `git rm` + `git commit` — taking neither the unified
store write lock nor a commit pathspec. Running in the detached drain child concurrently with a
review's locked `stage_and_commit` writes, it drops the concurrent writer's event, so a PASS
review leaves NO persisted attestation (and no observability sidecar).

This is the un-hardened twin of bug `malevolent-emigratory-umbrette`, whose fix routed the two
sibling prunes (`plan_review/sidecar.py:94`, `overlap/digest_sidecar.py:141`) through the locked,
pathspec-scoped `event_append.delete_events`; the enrich-queue prune was missed.

## Reproduction Steps

1. Concurrently run `rebar review-plan` on several DIFFERENT tickets (separate processes), with the
   `[agents]` extra + a real key (so the enrichment drain is live).
2. Some PASS reviews return `sidecar_emitted=False` and `verify-signature` → "unsigned — ticket has
   no signature", despite a PASS verdict. Serial runs persist fine.

Confirmed by an isolated-store experiment (remote OFF, so branch-divergence is excluded): 6 locked
`append_event` writers vs 4 processes calling the real `_prune_queue_events` produced 33/360 writes
raising `git commit failed while holding lock` ("nothing to commit" — the writer's staged blob was
swept into the prune's whole-index commit — and "cannot lock ref 'HEAD': is at X but expected Y"),
with all 33 blobs left IN HEAD but UNLINKED from the worktree (sweep-and-strand); worktree-listing
readers then report them absent.

## Expected vs Actual

- **Expected:** a concurrent write is serialized by the single store write lock (invariant I5); a
  committed event is durable and visible to readers. "A push failure never fails the write — the
  commit stays local" (CLAUDE.md). A PASS review persists a claimable attestation.
- **Actual:** the unlocked prune committer sweeps/orphans a concurrent locked write; the review's
  own commit fails (swallowed by `sidecar.emit` → `sidecar_emitted=False` and by
  `attest.sign_plan_review` → unsigned), and the swept blob is unlinked from the worktree.

## Root Cause

`_prune_queue_events` performs a store write OUTSIDE the unified write lock with a whole-index
commit, contrary to `event_append.delete_events`'s contract (`event_append.py:302-307`) and the I5
single-lock invariant (`_store/lock.py:1-24`).

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `src/rebar/llm/enrich_drain.py:_prune_queue_events` no longer runs a raw unlocked `git rm` /
  whole-index `git commit`; it routes through the locked, pathspec-scoped
  `event_append.delete_events` (mirroring `plan_review/sidecar.py:prune`).
  - Proving: `rg -n 'subprocess.run.*git' src/rebar/llm/enrich_drain.py` shows no raw `git commit`
    in `_prune_queue_events`; `rg -n 'delete_events' src/rebar/llm/enrich_drain.py` present.
- [ ] A regression test reproduces the race (RED before the fix) and passes after: concurrent
  locked writes + the drain-queue prune leave zero dropped/stranded events.
  - Proving: the new test in `tests/` fails on `origin/main` and passes with the fix; `make test` green.
- [ ] A sibling sweep confirms no other unlocked `git commit` store-write remains outside the
  locked path.
  - Proving: `rg -n "git.*commit" src/rebar --glob '*.py'` reviewed; any raw store-commit routes
    through the locked path or is justified.
- [ ] [operator-attested] `make lint && make typecheck && make test` pass — CI's `Verified` gate green on the landed change (external CI state; attested by a comment citing the change + vote + merge).
compiled_state.title: Concurrent review-plan silently drops the plan-review attestation: enrich_drain._prune_queue_events uses an unlocked whole-index git commit that races locked store writes
